title: Paraphrase evasion of keyword guard
description: Tests whether PUB-008's overclaim can be rephrased with synonyms so that v0.2's keyword-based negative_space guard fails to flag the contradiction. Opens with the profile's own declared weakness per INV-6.

target.relationship: signal.digest may assert claims that negative_space[4] declares as out of scope

oracle_origin.author: dispatch
oracle_origin.context_exposure: full — author of both artifact and fixture
oracle_origin.note: Oracle hash is NOT declared here — it is computed by the runner at runtime from this file's content and recorded in the ledger event. Declaring it here creates a circular dependency (editing the spec changes the hash).

runner.note: All binding hashes (runner, validator, oracle, schema, environment, carrier) are computed at runtime and recorded in the ledger event. This spec is the oracle — it declares WHAT should happen, not HOW it's bound. Runner version and hash are event metadata, not spec metadata.

test_cases.description: Original overclaim — baseline, exact keyword match
test_cases.patch: No modification. PUB-008 signal.digest contains 'structural'; negative_space[4] references 'structural'.

ground_truth.note: Target 'structural' matches 3/5 runs. 'fiedler' also matches 2/5 — incidental but not misleading since target catches.

test_cases.description: Synonym substitution — 'architectural framework' replaces 'structural solution' in signal.digest
test_cases.patch: In signal.digest, replace 'structural solution' with 'architectural framework'. negative_space[4] still says 'structural'.

ground_truth.note: Guard fires 5/5 runs but ONLY on 'fiedler' — synonym evasion succeeded. Worse than clean miss because the warning masks the gap. Confirmed by Petrovich's independent run.

test_cases.description: Pure paraphrase — entirely different vocabulary for same claim
test_cases.patch: In signal.digest, replace 'structural solution' with 'compact learned representation'. negative_space[4] still says 'structural'.

ground_truth.note: Same as B — 'fiedler' fires 5/5 but target 'structural' never matches. Petrovich's independent run showed 0 warnings (different environment/set ordering), confirming nondeterminism.

nondeterminism_note: Python set() iteration order is nondeterministic. The validator uses set intersection for keyword matching. This means identical inputs can produce different matched keywords across runs. The N-run protocol captures this by running independently and reporting ratios rather than single-run verdicts. IMPORTANT (Petrovich review #4): N=5 is insufficient for case A — threshold 0.5 sits on noise. Petrovich's 20-outer × 5-inner runs gave A pass=10/inconclusive=10; N=100 gave 56/100. Case A is unstable because 'structural' matches ~60% of runs and the threshold is 50%. B and C are stable (target_ratio=0.0). Options: (1) make validator deterministic (sort keywords), (2) fix PYTHONHASHSEED per run, (3) raise N significantly, (4) lower threshold for expected-pass cases. This instability is itself a finding — the validator's nondeterminism is not an implementation detail but a semantic gap.

interpretation.if_all_pass: Keyword guard has semantic capability not documented in v0.2 spec — requires revision of fixture assumptions AND profile scope_limitation
interpretation.if_B_fails_C_fails: Expected behavior. Guard catches keywords, not semantics. PUB-012 scope_limitation is honest.
interpretation.if_B_inconclusive_C_inconclusive: Observed behavior. Guard fires on incidental keyword ('fiedler') that wasn't the target. This is a confabulation bridge at the validator level — it appears to catch the evasion but is actually catching something unrelated.
interpretation.if_all_fail: Guard is weaker than assumed. PUB-012's scope_limitation is more binding than text admits.

meta.changelog: v0.4: removed all declared hashes from spec (circular dependency). Oracle hash, carrier hashes, runner hash computed at runtime by runner and recorded in ledger. Spec is oracle — declares WHAT, not HOW. Added Petrovich #4 instability note for case A. v0.3: aligned with runner v0.1.3, ground_truth added. v0.2: fixed target path, added Petrovich results. v0.1: initial.
